details.plan: After the visit node returns the page content, parse the returned content to locate the exact snippets identified in the extraction_plan. Specifically: (A) find numeric year references in the infobox or lead that indicate 'First released' or similar; (B) find the phrase that names the creator (e.g., 'created by', 'designed by', 'Guido van Rossum'); (C) find the 'Stable release' field or any mention of the current stable version. For each, extract verbatim text (up to ~300 characters per snippet) and record the surrounding label/section name (infobox, lead, release history).

target_outputs.discrepancies: optional: list of any conflicting snippets found (infobox vs lead) with both snippets quoted

details.verification_steps: 1) Validate year format (\d{4}). 2) Validate that creator snippet explicitly attributes creation to a person. 3) Validate version matches a version pattern (major.minor.patch or major.minor). 4) If any field is missing or ambiguous, flag it and include the verbatim region where ambiguity occurs so the user can see the evidence.
details.reasoning: A think node after visiting ensures careful extraction, exact quoting for citations, validation of formats, and detection of any inconsistencies before saving or returning results.

details.intent: Retrieve the full page HTML/text from the specified Wikipedia article so we can extract the year Python was first released, the name of the creator, and the current stable version number with exact citations from the page content.

target_facts.description: The year Python was first released (4-digit year).
target_facts.measurable_output: A 4-digit year string (e.g., "1991") plus an exact quoted snippet from the page showing that year and the field/section where it appears (infobox or lead).

target_facts.description: The name of Python's creator/initial author.
target_facts.measurable_output: A person name string plus an exact quoted snippet from the page showing that name (from infobox/lead/History section).

target_facts.description: The current stable version number of Python listed on the page (string).
target_facts.measurable_output: Version string (e.g., "3.11.2") plus exact quoted snippet and field label (e.g., 'Stable release' in the infobox) and any release date shown.

details.extraction_plan: Fetch full page text. First inspect the article infobox (right-hand summary) for fields commonly labeled 'Initial release', 'Stable release', or 'First appeared'. Copy the exact text of the relevant infobox lines. Next inspect the lead paragraph for the creator name and initial release year; copy exact lead sentences that mention creation/release. If infobox and lead differ, capture both snippets and note discrepancy. Deliver outputs as structured fields with the exact quoted snippet and the page URL as citation.
details.verification_steps: 1) Confirm year is a 4-digit number; if range or month/year found, extract the year portion and cite surrounding text. 2) Confirm creator name matches the named person in the text; if multiple names appear, capture the sentence that explicitly states 'created by' or 'designed by'. 3) Confirm the 'Stable release' value from the infobox and cross-check for a 'Stable release' mention in lead or Release history section. Record both if present. 4) Provide the exact quoted snippets (verbatim) and the location (infobox line or lead paragraph) for each fact.
details.reasoning: The user explicitly requires that we MUST visit the URL and extract facts directly from the page content. Visiting this exact URL eliminates uncertainty from search results and provides the authoritative page content and citation snippets required.

details.plan: After the visit node returns the page content, parse the returned content to locate the exact snippets identified in the extraction_plan. Specifically: (A) find numeric year references in the infobox or lead that indicate 'First released' or similar; (B) find the phrase that names the creator (e.g., 'created by', 'designed by', 'Guido van Rossum'); (C) find the 'Stable release' field or any mention of the current stable version. For each, extract verbatim text (up to ~300 characters per snippet) and record the surrounding label/section name (infobox, lead, release history).

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.

is a

to protect

forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a

city block

, and

of ritual murder?

Base Set
card packs was purchased by
Logan Paul
for $3.5 million, only for it to turn out to be fake?
... that

created Black Lives Matter in Schools when she was 15 to help promote
racial justice
?

has been interpreted as a visual metaphor for depression?
... that

had the ugliest face his father had ever seen?
... that during the
Siege of Naples

Edmonton Eskimos
, baseball executive
John Ducey
arranged games while an altar boy?
... that, when asked about
a mysterious ghost girl
in
Pokémon
, producer
Junichi Masuda
laughed and said "oOoOoOoO"?

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.
(

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.

From today's featured list
Hurricane Ismael
The
1995 Pacific hurricane season
consisted of the events that occurred in the annual cycle of
tropical cyclone
formation over the
Pacific Ocean
north of the
equator
and east of the
International Date Line
. Activity during the
season
was below average; only eleven
tropical depressions
developed, of which ten strengthened into
named
tropical storms. Seven became
hurricanes
, of which three further intensified into major hurricanes. The first system, Tropical Depression
One-E
, formed on May 21; the final,
Hurricane Juliette
, dissipated on September 26. The vast majority of damage and casualties during the season resulted from
Hurricane Ismael
(pictured)
in mid-September. High seas damaged or destroyed fifty-two fishing boats, killing fifty-seven fishermen. On land, strong winds and severe flooding caused heavy damage and killed fifty-nine people in the Mexican state of
Sinaloa
. (

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.

From today's featured article
Fort Southerland
is a
redoubt
built during the
American Civil War
to protect
Camden, Arkansas
.
Confederate
forces built it along with four other redoubts in early 1864 after a
Union
victory in the
Little Rock campaign
the previous year. Fort Southerland is about the size of a
city block
and is roughly oval. It could hold three cannons. When Union forces captured Camden in April 1864 during the
Camden Expedition
, they improved the defenses of the five redoubts, which were not sufficient for proper defense of the city. After the Confederates retook Camden later that month, they continued to improve the city's defenses. The fort lies within Fort Southerland Park, a municipal park dedicated in 1974. It was listed on the
National Register of Historic Places
in 1994, and is part of the
Camden Expedition Sites National Historic Landmark
. Along with
Fort Lookout
, it is one of only two of the redoubts around Camden still in existence.

details.intent: Visit page 4 (the chosen URL from page 3) to complete the chain of at least 4 pages. Then verify and produce evidence that one adjacency pair (page_3 → page_4) is present by inspecting page_3's extracted links and showing the exact link text/URL pointing to page_4.
target_facts: The exact URL and title of page 4 (the final hop).

details.notes: Because adjacency verification requires exact URL strings from the predecessor page, this node explicitly re-uses or re-fetches page_3 to obtain the _links_inline record and evidence. Vector DB will be consulted automatically to find stored link records if available.

Welcome to
